payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the November 2022 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.
payload.prepared_for: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.
Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.
Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.title: President, Metro-North Railroad and Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.description: Moving forward with procurement and construction packages for ADA upgrades at stations.
New LIRR terminal set to debut in 2022. New terminal at Grand Central providing LIRR service to and from Grand Central.
Double track expansion on the Ronkonkoma Branch.
Third track expansion on the Main Line.

GCM readiness project designed to provide much-needed additional train storage and operational flexibility in support of the new service. Work roared along in October towards its scheduled November signal cutover.

15-month installation project culminating with the October 29 and 30 signal cutover and subsequent testing. A 'ladder' is a series of switches that enables trains to cross quickly over several tracks. Now westbound Hempstead Branch trains can cross over Queens Interlocking and make regular station stops at Elmont's new north platform.

The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates. This state-of-good-repair effort will ensure the safety of the public and the railroad, improve commuter rail reliability, build more resilient infrastructure, and reduce noise and vibration levels by using modern materials. Major construction expected to begin in fall of 2023, repaired viaduct anticipated to be in service in 2027.

A public-private initiative created by the MTA and Partnership for New York City to bring private sector innovation to improve public transit. Recently announced winners of the COVID-19 Recovery and Sustainability Challenge that will deploy technologies to address safety and meet clean energy goals at both railroads over a one-year pilot.

warnings: project.partners is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency lists are relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.project_name: Metro-North Harlem Line ADA Accessibility Upgrades
payload.description: Two procurement and construction packages for ADA upgrades at six Metro-North stations along the Harlem Line. Package one already under construction will enhance accessibility at Hartsdale, Scarsdale, and Purdy's Station by constructing elevators, with project completion by next spring. Package two includes making Woodlawn and Williams Bridge Stations fully accessible and improving existing accessibility features at Botanical Gardens Station.

warnings: project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.description: ADA upgrades at nine LIRR stations. New elevators and upgrades scheduled to be installed at Locust Manor, St. Albans, Laurelton, Amityville, Copiague, Lindenhurst, Massapequa Park. Existing ADA components being upgraded at Auburndale and Valley Stream. Bundled into a single design-build contract.

warnings: project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.description: Preparing for future upgrades by initiating design to make 4 stations fully accessible: Forest Hills, Hollis, Douglaston and Cold Spring Harbor.

Metro-North partnered with the Building and Construction Trades Council of Greater New York and the Apprenticeship Readiness Collective to host a job fair at Tito Puente Educational Complex in East Harlem for the Park Avenue Viaduct project. Connected members of the East Harlem community with project-related construction jobs.
payload.location: Tito Puente Educational Complex, East Harlem
partners: Building and Construction Trades Council of Greater New York

location_normalized.raw_text: Tito Puente Educational Complex, East Harlem

warnings: project.partners is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency lists are relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Holiday Fair Ribbon Cutting

payload.event_name: Grand Central Holiday Fair Ribbon Cutting
payload.description: Ribbon cutting ceremony for the return of New York City's longest-running indoor holiday fair to Grand Central Terminal after a two-year hiatus.

Two hundred volunteers consisting of MTA employees, friends, and family participated in an exercise to navigate the concourse and make notes on travel paths throughout the terminal, signage, and general wayfinding elements at Grand Central Madison.

Over three weekends in October and November, cross-agency safety and preparedness drills took place at Grand Central Madison. Weekend one featured situational readiness drills in the tunnels, while weekend two prepared crews for various response scenarios within the station proper.

Metro-North Railroad commemorated Veteran's Day at its annual ceremony at Grand Central Terminal. President Cathy Rinaldi joined the MTA Police Department Pipe & Drum Band and members of the American Legion Commodore Vanderbilt Post 1158.

Metro-North conducted its federally-mandated annual emergency preparedness exercise in New Haven. The exercise simulated a fire scenario on Amtrak Acela equipment operating through Metro-North territory.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Job Fair in New Haven

payload.description: Metro-North held a job fair in New Haven focused on growing staff to budgeted targets across all crafts.
National Escalator Safety Week started November 14. LIRR, New York City Transit, Amtrak, and NJ TRANSIT spoke with customers in Penn Station to promote escalator safety.

tool_args.label: Bellerose Ladder enables Elmont-UBS full-time service

payload.claim_text: Once LIRR service to GCM begins at the end of the year, these new signals will make it possible for us to turn Elmont-UBS into a full-time station with service on the Hempstead Branch 365 days a year.
payload.subject: Elmont-UBS Station

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Three LIRR branches now serve Elmont-UBS

payload.claim_text: Islander fans and concert-goers now have the option of three LIRR branches - Hempstead, Port Jefferson/Huntington and Ronkonkoma - to take them directly to and from Elmont-UBS Station
payload.subject: Elmont-UBS Station service

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct carries 98% of Metro-North service

payload.claim_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct carries all of Metro-North's Grand Central Terminal trains, which comprise 98% of the service the railroad operates, but is a single point of failure.
payload.subject: Park Avenue Viaduct

tool_args.label: Over $5 billion in capital plan for ADA upgrades

payload.claim_text: More than $5 billion in the current Capital Plan is earmarked for Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility upgrade projects at stations, with $500 million of that dedicated for the commuter railroads.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Plan

payload.description: Partner in the Transit Tech Lab public-private initiative.
Participated in Metro-North annual emergency preparedness exercise. Also participated in National Escalator Safety Week at Penn Station with LIRR.

payload.entity_name: Joint Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad Committee
payload.description: Members include Samuel Chu (Co-Chair), Blanca Lopez (Co-Chair), Frank Borelli, Gerard Bringmann, Norman Brown, Michael Fleischer, Randy Glucksman, David Mack, Harold Porr III, Vinnie Tessitore, Midori Valdivia, Neal Zuckerman.

Transit Tech Lab winner from Sunnyvale, California, proposed to use LiDAR hardware on Metro-North paired with analytic software to report unsafe behavior, offer object detection, and measure passenger flows.

Transit Tech Lab winner from Seattle, proposed to provide cost-effective stormwater/climate-data and analytics for Metro-North to mitigate flooding, simplify monitoring, streamline maintenance and better deploy emergency resources.

Transit Tech Lab winner from New York City, proposed the use of wireless technology and web-based software to provide the LIRR and Metro-North with actionable opportunities to reduce energy consumption and optimize heating in buildings.
